title: MSC fleet sustains carrier strike groups during extended Iran-confrontation deployments
summary: Military Sealift Command fleet replenishment oilers and combat logistics ships continue to support extended US carrier strike group operations in the Red Sea and Arabian Sea, including USS Gerald R. Ford's record 309-day deployment, demonstrating sealift's enabling role for sustained naval presence against Iran.
contents:
# MSC Sustains Extended Carrier Strike Group Operations

## Overview
Military Sealift Command (MSC) fleet replenishment ships continued through April 30, 2026 to provide fuel, ammunition, and supplies to US carrier strike groups operating in the CENTCOM area of responsibility. Sustained underway replenishment supported USS Gerald R. Ford during its record 309-day Red Sea deployment as well as escorting destroyers and cruisers conducting blockade enforcement against Iran.

## Force Sustainment Contribution
- Fleet oilers (T-AO Henry J. Kaiser-class) deliver fuel underway to carriers and surface combatants
- T-AKE Lewis and Clark-class combat logistics ships transfer ammunition and dry stores
- Civilian-crewed ships free Navy personnel and tonnage for combat tasking
- Ports of call rotated through Diego Garcia, Bahrain, and Djibouti to keep tonnage forward

## Operational Picture
USS Gerald R. Ford and its strike group operated continuously in the Red Sea supporting US operations against Iran, accumulating 309 days at sea by April 12. USS Dwight D. Eisenhower completed sea trials on April 24 following extensive maintenance, while USS Theodore Roosevelt conducts workup training. The replacement pipeline relies on MSC tonnage to bridge transitions and keep carriers fueled and armed.

## Strategic Significance
The MSC contribution to the Iran confrontation underscores how a small civilian-crewed fleet of approximately 125 ships enables sustained US naval combat power. Without continuous fleet replenishment, carrier strike groups would have to rotate home for maintenance and resupply far sooner. MSC patterns also signal force generation readiness for additional contingencies.
